consistency.table_data_vs_description: The description accurately summarizes the table's function (how many spell slots are available per level, expending slots, and regaining slots on a long rest). The data in the table is a standard representation of Bard spell slot progression.
consistency.data_integrity: The spell slot counts within the table are mathematically consistent with standard D&D 5e Bard progression (e.g., full caster progression, reaching 4/3/2/1 slots for 9th level spells at level 17, and 4/3/3/3/3/2/2/1/1 at level 20).
consistency.formatting: The raw data uses an HTML table format, which is standard for representing structured tabular data in this context.

quality.completeness: The feature entry is complete, containing a slug, name, class, level, a detailed HTML table of progression, and a descriptive text block.
quality.clarity: The feature is very clear. The title and description explicitly state what the data represents (Bard Spell Slots per Level).
quality.relevance: Highly relevant for a Bard class entry, as spell slots are a core mechanic.

mechanics.rule_accuracy: The mechanics described (expending a slot of the spell's level or higher, regaining slots on a long rest) are mechanically accurate for D&D 5th Edition spellcasting classes like the Bard.
mechanics.progression_type: The feature correctly reflects a 'full caster' progression table, where spell slots are gained steadily across all 20 levels.
mechanics.level_mapping: The minimum level associated with the feature is 1, which is appropriate as spellcasting begins at level 1, even though the table spans 1 to 20.

llm_audit.archival_notes: This record is high quality and mechanically sound, representing the standard Bard spell slot progression table. The inclusion of the standard explanation for slot usage enhances its utility.
